danger_signals.visual_form: Polygonal 3D enemy models in flight space
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat requiring evasion or destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinctly-designed enemy craft at level endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Major objective encounter; progression gate requiring successful destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Planetary terrain, tunnel walls, environmental obstacles in flight path
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; player craft can collide but has limited damage tolerance
Immediate combat pressure; evasion or shield absorption required

safety_signals.meaning: Mission objective completion zone; safe progression marker
Objective marker; progression towards mission goal

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapon/tool selection UI indicating afterburner readiness
safety_signals.meaning: Evasion capability active; ability to flee danger at high speed (trade-off: cannot fire)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Polygonal enemy spacecraft designs that visually represent the ASFAR fleet
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Planetary environments where ASFAR forces operate; spacecraft presence in space

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player's TV-202 starfighter; distinct from enemy craft visual design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled vehicle in mission spaces; sole ally craft

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Located on Proxima Seven; likely represented through environmental design and final boss encounter
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Proxima Seven planet; supercomputer facility environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Warship presences, combat encounters across planetary environments, destroyed or militarized worlds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy forces actively engage player; multiple hostile encounters per mission; boss enemies control planetary access

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-revelation (Episode 3): X.I. supercomputer revealed as true antagonist
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to Proxima Seven environment; shift in mission focus from general alien forces to computational threat elimination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final mission objectives target supercomputer facility; narrative-driven progression change

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unknown nearby planet displaying visual transformation; environmental anomalies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Investigation and destruction of transformative force; reveals Sy Wickens connection to X.I.

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Immersive polygonal 3D environments rendered at high frame rate; sense of velocity through visual motion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player engages in fast-paced action sequences; terrain proximity flying; evasion-focused gameplay

environmental_storytelling.example: Nine distinct planetary environments (three per episode) communicate different world-states and conflict zones within ASFAR-Earth war
Tunnel entrances/exits, checkpoints, and extraction points create mission narrative flow and objective hierarchy
Final boss at end of each planet signals climactic confrontation and progression gate; visual design distinguishes it as major threat
Rewarded exploration mechanic; reveals world has hidden spaces communicating depth and player agency
Low-altitude flight over terrain visually communicates danger through proximity to environmental hazards; reinforces high-risk combat setting

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can change course instantly; no inertia; capable of low-speed flight without falling; survives some terrain collisions and damage hits
Moves at very high speed for evasion; temporarily sacrifices ability to return fire; communicates risk/reward trade-off
Engage player with projectile fire; patrol/intercept in mission spaces; represent constant threat requiring destruction
Must be destroyed to proceed to next planet or complete episode; represents scaled-up threat with distinct visual presentation
Presents collision danger; forces evasion gameplay; communicates environment as active threat alongside enemy forces

notes: Terminal Velocity uses 1995-era 3D polygon graphics to communicate world-state through environmental design, enemy visual variety, and mission structure. The game's 'fast, high-energy action' is visually reinforced through immersive low-level flight perspectives and high-speed movement sensation. Danger is signaled through enemy presence and terrain proximity; safety through objective markers and extraction points. The narrative shift from ASFAR conflict to X.I. supercomputer threat likely manifests in visual environment transition to Proxima Seven. Limited direct documentation on specific color schemes, sigils, or detailed faction visual markers in available source material; extraction based on gameplay paradigm as combat flight simulator with multiple planetary environments and enemy types.
